title: Case Viability Screener Agent
shortDescription: Scores each new matter against your firm's case-acceptance criteria and surfaces the borderline ones for partner judgment.

seo.title: AI Case Viability Screener for Law Firms | Dodonai
seo.description: Score every new matter against your firm's acceptance criteria. Borderline cases surface for partner review before billable hours get spent.

hero.headline: AI Case Viability Screener Agent
hero.subheadline: Every new matter scored against your firm's written criteria. Strong cases fast-tracked, borderline ones flagged for partner review, weak ones declined politely. The judgment work happens before the workup hours start.
bullets: Lead scoring tuned to your firm's specific acceptance criteria
Estimated case value range based on similar past matters
Recommended disposition with the reasoning per criterion
Borderline cases routed to partner review automatically

scenario.heading: The case you would have worked up for nothing
paragraphs: A new personal injury matter comes in. Slip and fall at a small commercial property, soft-tissue injuries, treatment ongoing. Your associate is about to spend 4 hours pulling the police report, ordering medical records, and drafting an evaluation memo. The Case Viability Screener flags it first: comparable matters in your archive settled in the $4,000 to $8,000 range; the property's insurance carrier disputes nearly every soft-tissue claim under $15,000; the case scores 38 against your acceptance criteria. The recommendation is decline. The partner agrees in 2 minutes. The 4 hours of workup never happens.

whatItDoes.heading: What the Case Viability Screener does
bullets: Extracts the case facts from the intake form, supporting documents, and any uploaded medical or accident records
Scores the matter against the criteria your partners use today (injury severity, liability clarity, insurance coverage, jurisdictional venue, practice fit)
Pulls comparable past matters from your archive to estimate a value range with the basis cited
Produces a 1-page evaluation memo with the recommendation and the reasoning per criterion
Routes borderline scores (typically 50 to 70) to partner review rather than auto-declining

integrations: Clio, MyCase, Practice Panther, or your PMS
Document management system for the prior matter archive
Slack or email for the partner-review queue

safety.heading: Safety posture
paragraphs: The screener recommends; the attorney decides. It never auto-declines a matter or auto-signs an engagement letter. The acceptance criteria live in a file your partners can edit, so the judgment encoded in the agent is your firm's judgment, not a vendor's. Every score is logged with the inputs and the per-criterion reasoning so the call is auditable.

faq.question: How does it know what our acceptance criteria actually are?
faq.answer: The Build phase starts with a working session where your partners articulate the criteria you weigh today. We encode those into a skill file you can review and edit. After the first 20 matters, we calibrate the scoring against the calls your partners would have made to catch any drift.

faq.question: What if our criteria change as the practice evolves?
faq.answer: The criteria file is editable by your team. A partner who wants to tighten the bar on borderline soft-tissue cases can adjust the weights and the threshold; the next intake reflects the change immediately. The Managed retainer includes quarterly criteria reviews to surface drift and validate the calibration.

faq.question: Does it work for non-PI practices?
faq.answer: Yes. The same pattern applies to family law (custody complexity, asset profile, conflict potential), estates work (estate size, family dynamics, potential will contests), and other practice areas. The criteria are different; the architecture is the same.
